headline: Submarine Cable Vulnerability Threatens Malaysian Internet
context: Risk assessment found 95% of international internet traffic passes through just 4 vulnerable cable landing points.

cards.big: 95% of internet traffic through only 4 vulnerable cable landing points
cards.sub: Submarine cable damage could sever international connectivity for millions

cards.big: 2 cable incidents in 2025 reduced bandwidth 35% for 72 hours each
cards.sub: Ship anchor damage near Mersing affected 8.5 million users

cards.big: Malaysia connects to 24 cables but 18 land at Mersing or Kuantan
cards.sub: Geographic concentration means single event could disable majority of traffic

cards.big: RM1.8 billion diversification to 3 new sites remains in planning
cards.sub: New landings in Sarawak Sabah and Penang would distribute risk

cards.big: Cable concentration tests whether internet resilience receives infrastructure-level investment
Submarine cable vulnerability threatens connectivity. A single cable cut could isolate Malaysian internet for days.
